subject: Claude Code v2.1.210
object: Added a startup warning for Write(path) , NotebookEdit(path) , and Glob(path) permission rules — use Edit(path) or Read(path) instead

subject: Google 因 AI 训练再遭出版商集体
object: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers

subject: Google 因 AI 训练再遭出版商集体
object: A group of publishers and authors have filed a class action lawsuit against Google, accusing the tech giant of using their copyrighted works to train its AI platform, Gemini.

subject: Google 因 AI 训练再遭出版商集体
object: The group of plaintiffs, which includes Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, author Scott Turow, and S.C.R.I.B.E., also alleges that Google intentionally removed or changed copyright information on these works to “conceal… that its Gemini Models were trained on stolen materials,” according to the lawsuit.

subject: Google 因 AI 训练再遭出版商集体
object: This lawsuit is just one of many complaints that publishers, authors, and other copyright holders have filed against AI companies such as Google, Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic.

subject: Announcing our $22 Million
object: Today, we’re announcing $27 million in combined Seed and Series A funding, with the Series A led by CRV alongside Y Combinator, Pathlight Ventures, and angels including the founders of Altruist and Jump AI. This funding gives us the resources to go deeper into the mission we started with: making compliance effortless for regulated financial firms.

subject: Sam Altman didn’t need another
object: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit

subject: Sam Altman didn’t need another
object: On Friday, OpenAI was hit with a high-profile lawsuit from Apple, over allegedly stealing trade secrets. OpenAI’s expensive hardware bet is what’s on the line.

subject: Sam Altman didn’t need another
object: OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world’s richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile legal actions yet — from Apple. OpenAI’s expensive hardware bet is what’s on the line.

subject: Sam Altman didn’t need another
object: Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI, filed in Northern California federal court, accused former Apple employees of “stealing Apple’s trade secrets for the benefit of OpenAI.” The 41-page complaint states that Apple keeps its “product development, manufacturing, supply chain, technology research, and other innovations confidential” and that “the trade secrets spanning Apple’s hardware operations collectively constitute one of the most valuable intellectual assets in all of American business.”
